comments.comment: Based on stack testing, Sulphur Dioxide in 2021 had an emission rate of 1.2 g/s compared to 2022 the rate of sulphur dioxide was 2.58 g/s.  When easily degraded organic material is in the sewer, dissolved oxygen is low and bacteria use sulphates as a source of oxygen and reduce the sulphate to sulphur.  The sulphates would normally flow out of the plant but the sulphur compounds can sink and end up in the sludge being incinerated and turn into sulphur dioxide.
